blocks.content: Maya stared blankly at Kaiden's strange behavior.
Kaiden, lifting his head cautiously, noticed his right hand acting out again and quickly pulled his arm back.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Damn it! This is driving me crazy.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Kaiden, are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Oh, um…
There was nothing he could say.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: What's going on?
Other residents sharing the same corridor emerged into the hallway, and Reina, the leader of the troupe, entered the room.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: What's the matter? What happened?
Everyone's gaze fell on Kaiden, who was sitting under a blanket, his face burning with embarrassment.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Hm.
Having grown up with brothers, Reina could guess what had happened.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I thought something serious had happened. Maya, don't worry about it. He's just healthy, that's all. But Kaiden, please be mindful of the noise at night.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: No, that"s not it.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Kaiden wanted to salvage the situation, but his tongue failed him under the scrutiny of so many people.
Reina gently pulled Maya by the shoulder and headed for the door.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Let's go, Maya.
As they walked out, she whispered,

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I know you're old classmates, but be careful. I've been watching him, and honestly, he seems a bit shady… maybe even perverted.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Ah, okay.
Maya wanted to deny it, but she couldn't be sure herself.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: All right, everyone, let's head back to our rooms. We've got roll call in the morning, so get some rest.
As Reina dispersed the group, Maya glanced back at Kaiden's door in the now-empty hallway.
Meanwhile, Kaiden hurriedly pulled up his pants, nearly in tears.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Damn it. You idiot.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: He thought it might have been better to run through the streets of Jive naked than to endure this.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: As long as Maya wasn't there to see it.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: I should go back to Tormia. What kind of knight am I? Staying here will only cause trouble for everyone.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: He felt like someone destined to sit alone in a gloomy room, endlessly sketching the face of the woman he loved.
A knock, followed by Maya's voice.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Can I come in for a moment?

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Not now. How could I face her?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I really need to tell you something. It's important.
When Kaiden opened the door, Maya entered with a calm smile.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: How's your hand?

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Huh? Oh…
Kaiden sighed as he looked at his troublesome right hand, then spoke honestly.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: I'm sorry. It's what you think it is. I made a fool of myself. I don't even know how to apologize…

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: It's fine. It's not the first time.
Maya knew about Kaiden's uncontrollable right hand.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: What's your true intention?

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Huh?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You're always kind to me, but your right hand always seems to have its own… inappropriate ideas. Honestly, I don't know what you want.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: No way! Inappropriate thoughts? I'd never dream of such a thing!
Kaiden waved both hands, but only his left moved. His right hand formed an 'OK' sign with his thumb and forefinger.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Would you stop already!

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Kaiden yelled, hitting his right arm.
As another battle between his hands broke out, Maya spoke.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Kaiden, I've been thinking a lot since your confession. But honestly, I… I still can't forget Shirone.
Kaiden's right arm fell limp.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Whenever Shirone's name came up, his right arm would always go still.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: I-I see. I wasn't expecting anything anyway…

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: But,

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'm trying to move on. Shirone has Amy, after all. I can't cling to him forever, can I?

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Kaiden's heart raced.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: I can't make any promises. But… can you give me a little time? Would that be too much to ask?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: No, not at all. I can wait. I'll wait forever… no, for eternity if I have to.
It was a foolishly grand answer, but Maya smiled.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Thank you for always caring about me. Good night.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Oh, yeah. You too…
After Maya left, Kaiden sat frozen for a long time before collapsing onto his bed.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: She's just pitying me.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: How pathetic and pitiful he must have seemed.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Ugh.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: His eyes welled up.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: But she didn't say no.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: For someone crushed by the weight of the Red Cross Star's destiny, it was the first glimmer of hope Kaiden had ever known.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: She said it's okay to wait.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: He felt himself breaking free from the confines set by God.

blocks.speaker: Kaiden
blocks.content: Maybe… maybe I can do it.
Through his tear-blurred vision, his right hand moved closer and gently covered his eyes.
The incident in the artist's residence was a minor mishap, but Captain Rai of the guard was on edge.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: No sleep for me tonight.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: Not that he was planning to sleep anyway.
As he passed through the artist's district into the teaching area, he saw someone approaching in the distance.
Even from the limp in their gait, Rai recognized them.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: Parka Kuan.
Rai stood straight and saluted as Kuan approached. Kuan gave a brief nod in return.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: Where have you been?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I just went for a walk to clear my head.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: You don't have to be so formal. It makes me uncomfortable.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I hope I'm not being presumptuous, but I heard you had a reason. Even without that, I think you'd be better suited to the role than I am.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: Siena.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: Since arriving at the sanctuary, Kuan hadn't met her even once.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: A one-eyed, one-armed, limping man.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: What dreams could those with intact bodies expect him to have?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Do your best.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: As Kuan walked away, he muttered,

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: A one-armed man cannot fight to protect others.
By the time Rai turned around, Kuan had disappeared from sight.

blocks.speaker: Captain Rai
blocks.content: Heretic!
Vanisa, restrained by the Miracle Stream, shouted, his jaw dislocated and hanging loosely.
Shirone swung the light's energy in a full arc, slamming Vanisa into the wall with a loud thud.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oops!

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: If he had been an ordinary human, every bone in his body would have shattered.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Ughhhhh…

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Despite his broken form, Vanisa rose to his feet.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: He's a Denier.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Trapped in the prison of his flesh, he had transcended death.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Unlike Rian, however, his body did not regenerate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Stop,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Not being able to die doesn't mean you're alive. If you keep destroying your body like this, you'll just become a lump of meat.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Foolish wretch,

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Nothing will change.
Charging again, now more like a monster than a man, Vanisa's distorted figure was once again engulfed by the light's energy.
Gaaahhh!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wherever the light touched, black ash scattered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As expected, it doesn't purify.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It only burned away, leaving nothing behind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If the light of Agape continued to pour into him… would there be anything left of him at all?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Give up. You've turned into a demon in human flesh. For demons, purification means annihilation.
Graaagh!
Vanisa's ribs all erupted from his body, extending like spider legs, with one broken rib hanging uselessly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Flesh, mind, spirit, emotions…

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: We exist only for the glory of God!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Damn it!
The light of Agape exploded, and Vanisa was hurled into the darkness of the corridor.
Phew.
Wiping the sweat from under his chin, Shirone approached to find Vanisa's body charred like coal.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Ahhh…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where… am I?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In prison,

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: Does this mean… I can leave now?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I guess not. God has not forgiven me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This was the third immortal Shirone had encountered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Why can't they die?' Rian, Gaold, and now Vanisa.

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: I see it, Yahweh,

blocks.speaker: Vanisa
blocks.content: God has determined your end. You will never win. You'll shed tears of blood… hahaha.
With a crumble, the muscles of his face, now coal-black, broke apart.
Grk\u2026 grrk\u2026
Shirone stared at the lifeless body, which could no longer convey anything, and slowly turned away.
A wailing sound echoed through the air.
ΣΣΣ
By 4:30 AM, lights flickered on one by one in the Delta headquarters.
By 5:00 AM, the corridors buzzed with activity as delegations from various departments of the sanctuary arrived in Delta.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Ugh, so uncomfortable.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Seriel from the World Health Organization stretched and spotted a familiar face in the distance.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Huh?

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: Wow! Amira!

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: Oh, Seriel! It's been ages. I've heard about you.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: Hello, nice to meet you.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: Dorothy was Iruki's representative, while Liz attended the sanctuary as a director of Neid's group.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: So, is Iruki really not coming? It's bound to come up during the public speech,

blocks.speaker: Liz
blocks.content: I don't know. There's no word from him. Shirone hasn't said anything, so I suppose it's fine, but…

blocks.speaker: Liz
blocks.content: Don't worry. He's probably just goofing off somewhere strange. That's what guys like him do.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: As small talk continued, Seriel scanned the room.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Amy isn't here?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Then her expression stiffened.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Well, well, look who it is.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It was Fermi, sipping coffee as he approached.

blocks.speaker: Liz
blocks.content: Long time no see. Still as shifty as ever? You've finally wormed your way into politics after all that scheming at school, huh?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Haha! If it helps the world, I'll gladly throw myself into the fire.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: He winked at Seriel.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I read your paper. Your analysis on emotional disorders was excellent.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: …What are you doing right now?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Why are you talking to me? Do you think I've forgotten what you did? You turned countless people into drug addicts.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Which is why I'm talking to you. I've got a great idea…
As Fermi reached to place a hand on her shoulder, Seriel swatted his arm away.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Don't touch me.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Still as cold as ever.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Just like back in school.
ΣΣΣ
The staff member from Jive approached.

blocks.speaker: Staff member
blocks.content: The sanctuary's official event is about to begin. Attendees, please make your way to the main hall.
The women dispersed to their respective departments, and as Seriel passed Fermi, she ground her teeth and said,

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: You. Meet me later.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Still as cold as ever.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Just like back in school.
